content.subtitle: Use
rows: To express a wish about the present or a general situation
Maria wishes she **lived** near the sea.
I wish I **wasn't** so nervous before exams.
To express regret about something that happened in the past
He wishes he **had listened** to his teacher's advice.
I wish we **hadn't stayed** out so late last night.
To express annoyance about someone's behaviour, or to complain
I wish the neighbours **would stop** making so much noise.
I wish you **wouldn't leave** your things everywhere.
To express wishes about what is possible now or in the future
She wishes she **could play** the piano.
I wish I **could stay** longer, but I have to leave.

content.subtitle: Extra notes
items: With wish + past simple, both was and were are acceptable for I/he/she/it. Were is the more formal option: I wish I was/were a better cook.
If only can replace wish with the same meaning: If only I was/were a better cook.

content.subtitle: Be careful
items: We don't use would when wishing for something about ourselves: I wish I knew the answer. (not I wish I would know the answer.)
For situations that might really happen in the future, use hope instead of wish: I hope the weather is nice tomorrow.

sections.title: C: Original course practice
sections.instructions: Complete each original item using the target language from this lesson.

items.sentence: During the class project on Wish, Mia wrote ...... to complete the model sentence.
items.answer: I wish I had a bike.

items.sentence: In an original course practice item about Wish, the best completion is ...... .
items.answer: I wish my brother would tidy his room.

items.sentence: Sara checked the course practice notes and added ...... to the example.
items.answer: I wish I had brought my dictionary yesterday.

items.sentence: The online lesson included a short sentence about Wish: the missing part is ...... .
items.answer: I wish I could stay out late.

items.sentence: During the school club on Wish, Nina wrote ...... to complete the model sentence.
items.answer: I hope it doesn't snow tomorrow.

items.sentence: In an original course practice item about Wish, the best completion is ...... .
items.answer: If only I was an astronaut./If only I were an astronaut.

items.answer: I wish I had passed the test yesterday.

items.sentence: In an original course practice item about Wish, the best completion is ...... .
items.answer: I hope it doesn't rain tomorrow.

sections.title: E: Original key word transformation practice
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it keeps the same meaning as the first.

items.context: Mia wrote an original reference sentence about Wish during the class project.
items.sentence:
Mia began the class project before the lesson on Wish. **HAD**
The class project ...... before the lesson on Wish.
